Standing Steel is the number one. Number one, I'm not going to go from a hundred down. I'm going to give you the first one. Not doing anything, inactivity is the worst possible thing you can do as a real estate investor. Often you freeze because you don't have the experience. You're not sure about a deal. You don't know how to analyze. You don't trust your own feelings about a deal. You don't have the necessary intel or disregard it. You don't ask the right questions. You don't have the insight to confidently move forward. And even when you do, you don't pull the trigger. Look, no matter the situation or why, when you do nothing, that's a problem. This is the number one mistake I made in real estate. The first three years, well, the first mistake I made was that little baby deal. The second bigger mistake, much bigger mistake, was those three years where I never bought anything while I was in Houston, Texas. I shopped. I collected data. I read. I researched. I talked. And I bought. I first started in real estate, as you know, investing 25 years ago. I lived in Houston. I first tried to get involved in real estate investing when I lived there, but never closed a deal. So I need to rephrase that. I looked, but I didn't buy. You're not investing if you don't purchase. I never closed a deal because I always found a reason not to buy, the economy, the interest rates, the job report, presidential election. I just found reason after reason. I talked to too many people. My uncle influenced me not to. My mom said it was going to be a problem. My brother thought I had too much going on. Everybody knew what I should do, but I knew I should be buying this property and didn't. To this day, it's one of the biggest regrets of my life. Today at Cardone Capital. We own almost 5,000 units. By the time you get this, it could be closer to 7,000 units. And I still wonder, had I not been so conservative in those early years starting in Houston, then when I went to San Diego, how many units would I have today? I could easily today. Had I not made this first mistake, I could easily be one of the top 10 apartment owners in the United States today, worth billions of dollars. To learn from my mistakes, first off, don't stand still. So mistake number one is not investing. This is the biggest mistake you can make. Not having money in real estate is the biggest mistake you will make for yourself and your family. You can't be a real estate investor until you invest. Until then, your talk, your learning learn, you're at the conventions, at the seminars, you're collecting data, but you're not doing anything. Look, I'm not interested in talk. I'm interested in action. How much you study, how much you learn will not get you a deal. You have to pull the trigger. Whether you invest with your own money, with a partner's capital, or with financing, pull the trigger. Do your research, find a great deal, and move to the future. Figure out how to get money into apartment buildings today. If you could figure out how to do it yesterday, I would tell you to do that. You know the old saying, when's the best time to plant a tree? If you didn't plant it 20 years ago, plant it today. Apartment buildings will produce massive passive income when bought correctly. And that's the goal. Any property that produces massive cash flow today, passive income today, and can be held over long periods of time, when bought in the right locations at the right scale, will provide and achieve you meaningful, long-term financial freedom. Mistake number two, not knowing your market. You need to know your market. You don't need to shop outside your market until you know your market. You need to know every sale on the market. You need to know every comp in the market. You need to know every sale comp and rent cop in the market. Also, you need to know the comparables on vacancy in the market, expenses超 mention market, salaries in the market, jobs in the market. Hey, you need to know everything. Did I say that? You need to know every rental in the market. You need to know what's being under rented and over leveraged. You need to know what sold in the last few cycles, every vacancy, every possible expense that hits these buildings. You need to know where you need to hire security, what it costs, and when you don't need security. You need to know that now and you need to go look forward slightly into the future. If you don't know your market to that extent, then you don't know your market. And that's a problem. The first part of that problem is not poor investing. The first part of that problem is you're not going to do anything because you know the ultimate no. You don't know. NO means no. And KNOW, you don't know, means no deals. That's what keeps high potential real estate investors, high potential from being real investors. Dive in and make some money, but you have to know. Don't be that person that doesn't invest, but goes to the conference. Know your market inside and out. State local to your market so you have the confidence, the security, the certainty to move forward and lock down the right deals the minute they hit the market. Even overpay if you need to. When you know a market, you can't pay too much if you know the market. Mistake number three, not dealing with the dollars and cents of it all. You don't have millions of dollars kicking around to invest in apartment buildings because let's face it, you haven't been paying attention to the dollars and the cents. Many investors are in the same situation as you are. Don't let a lack of cash stand in your way of finding deals and figuring out how to make them work. Find a financing arm that can provide you with great terms and the confidence you need to close your deals. Elon Musk does not have the money it takes to go to Mars. You like Elon? He's going to figure out how to go to Mars even though he doesn't have the money because Elon is committed to go to Mars. You have a commitment problem. You're talking about the money you don't have rather than the commitment that you need to have so you can figure out how to get the money. Look I had to go to, like I told you, I had to go to three lenders to get money on my first deal but I kept pushing because I was committed. I got the backing I needed to move forward. That deal by the way, I put $350,000 in that deal and made $4 million. It was the first score of my entire life at 35 years old. It was an incredible day, an incredible lesson to know I could persist to get the debt because I was committed and then got rewarded three or four or five years later with a big score. To piggyback on that, you can't fear debt. In this business debt is good. If it wasn't for debt I would not be able to do big deals today and by the way almost no one else would either. Then the guys that are paying cash are using money, they're using money from pension plans that they got from investors to pay for their deals in cash and then they promised to pay the pension or the investors in the pension back a small percentage. I have barred more than $400 million in debt, all of which has been paid for mostly by the properties. I get that not every investor agrees with me on this debt thing. They're wrong. I'm not wrong on this. You try to pillar's right, Coca-Cola's right, Apple's right, Elon Musk is right, the big boys use debt. Even Goldman Sachs, when they get money, when a bank gets money, when Wells Fargo gets money or Bank of America, when you put money into their account what do they do with it? They get rid of it as soon as you give it to them and they lend it out to the marketplace so they can multiply. This is leverage. You have to learn how to use it. I can't tell you the number of times the debt I had on a deal put a deal, made a deal possible and more attractive to me and even the debt I had put on it when I went to sell it, made that deal more attractive when I sold it because the new buyer assumed my debt. Get this now. When you have confidence in your financing, not doubt, you will be able to close big deals because you're going to be able to give the seller the assurance and the broker the assurance that you can deliver. Today I have several banks and lenders I tap into to close my deals and we work on growing that list as we can because we want more people to lend me money, not less. Now I see that the debt and financing are my friends. They're my partner. They're not the enemy. They're not the bad guy. Get there now and you'll be able to, you'll be, get there. Get this in your head now and you'll be in a better position to move forward on the big deals. Mistake number four, not buying through every cycle. This comes back to the notion of not investing at all. To be a serious real estate investor, you need to buy through all cycles, down cycles, flat cycles, up cycles. Even at the top of a cycle, you need to be willing to buy deals. You can't just wait and buy at the bottom. Nobody does that. If you wait, you're buying Orlando, let's say, or Houston or Sacramento or Denver, pick your city and you're trying to buy at the bottom. Guess what? When that market bottoms out and you haven't been buying as it went down, you won't even be considered because you hadn't bought anything. What broker do you know? What debt arm do you have? What are you going to do? Call somebody. Hey, I hadn't bought a deal in like seven years, but I'm in the market now. Yeah, dude, you're at the bottom of the list. You're going to do a deal with me because I bought through the entire cycle. Buy at the bottom, buy at the middle. How bullish am I on apartments? I buy at the bottom, I buy at the middle, I buy on the way up, I buy on the way down. Hell, I'll buy at the damn top. You know why? Because time, time stretches out the top, right? Time reduces the bottoms. As long as I got five years, seven years, 10 years, 12 years, 15 years, buy great properties and great locations that provide cash flow, that property will make a new top. So whether you buy at the bottom, the middle of the top, you've got to be in the market, you've got to be looking, you've got to be making offers, you've got to be active. Like I say, if they don't know you, they won't flow you. And in this case, it means flowing you with deals and flowing you with financing.
